checklist.claim: The five deferral titles emitted by the helper are byte-identical to the removed inline case arms
checklist.evidence: arm-by-arm diff of removed vs new; corroborated by code-reviewer + final-pass

checklist.claim: The workflow invokes the helper leading-token and verifies the outcome (TITLE non-empty), never silently dropping the title
checklist.evidence: silent-failure-hunter traced every failure mode → fail-closed breadcrumb

checklist.claim: The run.sh #389 block drives all 5 arms and pins source arm-order so a reorder/deletion turns RED
checklist.evidence: pr-test-analyzer confirmed; mutation-check proved reorder→RED, delete→RED

checklist.claim: The four pre-existing #353/#304 title pins are repointed to the helper in the same commit (no stale workflow-title pins)
checklist.evidence: code-reviewer + pr-test-analyzer confirmed no orphaned pins remain

checklist.claim: AC4 changeset present with bump: patch, type Changed, no direct plugin.json/CHANGELOG edit

phase3_findings.description: helper header honesty-rule prose re-enumerates the four reason names + * default (mirror-fact, low rot risk, co-located with the case)
the workflow fallback repeats the helper's generic title as a substring; the 'defined once' pin covers only the helper, so a coupled pin could catch drift

fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion below important threshold; comment-analyzer said 'no change required' — enumeration is intrinsic to the honesty rationale and co-located with the case; run.sh arm-order pin catches a case-side change

fix_decisions.claim_text: workflow fallback prefix not coupled-pinned to helper generic title
fix_decisions.evidence: Suggestion below important threshold; the two strings are independent honest-generic fallbacks (deliberately byte-distinct per silent-failure-hunter), not a required coupled invariant — pinning would over-constrain; low stakes (fallback fires only when helper absent/broken)
